title: CI/CD Pipeline Deployment Rollback After Database Migration Failure
symptoms: Application instances throwing SQL errors for undefined column references
Rolling deployment showing 40% of instances in failed health checks
Database migration log showing column drop completed successfully
Old application version instances unable to query the modified table
Deployment pipeline stuck in partial rollback state
root_cause: Database migration dropped a column before all application instances were updated to the new version that no longer references it, violating the expand-and-contract migration pattern
resolution: Manually re-added the dropped column to the database, completed the application rollback, re-planned the migration using expand-and-contract pattern with separate deploy phases
preventive_actions: Enforce expand-and-contract pattern for all breaking schema changes
Implement database migration pre-check against running application versions
Add deployment canary phase with automatic rollback triggers
Create database migration rollback scripts for every migration
Implement blue-green deployment for breaking changes

timeline.event: Production deployment pipeline initiated in Jenkins
Database migration executes, drops user_legacy_id column
Rolling deployment begins, new instances starting
Old instances begin failing with SQL column not found errors
P1 alert fires, 40% of instances in failed state
Rollback blocked by irreversible migration, manual intervention needed
Dropped column manually re-added to database
Application rollback completed, all instances healthy

embedding_summary: CI/CD deployment rollback after database migration dropped a column still referenced by the previous application version. 40% of instances failed during rolling deployment. 55-minute partial outage affecting 15,000 users. Resolved by manually restoring the column and completing rollback.
